ul.li: Host: Input/output buffers + 2 pinned staging buffers
Device: 2 input buffers + intermediate + output + window + work area
No ring buffers: Direct pipeline flow
Host: Input/output buffers + pinned ring buffers (98 KB, DMA via peek_frame_at_offset())
Device: Scaled by max_frames_per_submit (~490 KB for max_frames=5)
Ring buffers: Per-channel pinned memory — zero-copy staggered DMA (Opt 3.1)
Batched path: d_window_ + lazy cuFFT plan cache for direct kernel launches
Use case: Real-time streaming with arbitrary chunk sizes

div.p: Device buffers are larger than BatchExecutor due to max_frames scaling. Memory tradeoff: higher device allocation enables 2-6× latency reduction.
StreamingExecutor Mode: Low-latency streaming, arbitrary chunk sizes Total Memory: ~590 KB (nfft=2048, channels=2, max_frames=5) +260% vs BatchExecutor

ul.li: Ring buffers in pinned memory — zero-copy DMA via peek_frame() / peek_frame_at_offset()
Optimization 3.1: Device buffers scaled by max_frames_per_submit for batched processing
Batched cuFFT plans cached lazily (keyed by N×channels)
Ring buffers: 3 × nfft × channels × sizeof(float) = 98 KB (host, pinned)
Device input: nfft × channels × max_frames × sizeof(float) × 2 buffers = 160 KB
Device intermediate: (nfft/2+1) × channels × max_frames × sizeof(complex) = 164 KB
Device output: (nfft/2+1) × channels × max_frames × sizeof(float) = 82 KB
cuFFT work areas: dynamic (1-2 cached plans)

div.p: Tradeoffs: ✅ Supports arbitrary input chunk sizes ✅ Continuous streaming capability ✅ Per-channel independence ✅ Zero-copy H2D DMA via peek_frame_at_offset() (Opt 3.1) ✅ 1 sync per submit instead of N (Opt 3.1) ❌ Higher memory overhead (device buffers × max_frames)

ol.li: peek_frame_at_offset(nfft, i×hop) - Staggered zero-copy (N≥2 batched path)
advance(N × hop_size) - Bulk advance after single sync

div.p: Batched Device Layout (N=4, 2 channels): d_input: [ch0_f0, ch1_f0, ch0_f1, ch1_f1, ch0_f2, ch1_f2, ch0_f3, ch1_f3] D2H: copies only [ch0_f3_mag, ch1_f3_mag] (last frame)

ul.li: D2H: Only last frame copied back
Opt 3.1: Lazy plan cache (keyed by N×channels)
Typical: 1-2 cached plans in steady state

text.tspan: (DMA all frames from pinned ring buf)
